description:
# Why

bl-ffb4's open architectural question:

> Plugins on the mastered side: a clone of B with master_url=A inherits A's plugin configs from A's balls/tasks, not B's committed config. Need to nail down precedence.

The user's answer (2026-05-20): **master wins. Unified config.**

This is the natural composition with the rest of the multi-repo model: the bridge/proxy pattern (bl-bfb7) already centralizes plugin policy in one place — the bridge clone. Letting client repos override would re-introduce the very fragmentation (N secrets, N sync races, drifting mirroring policy) that the bridge pattern exists to eliminate. Master-wins also matches the field's own semantics: master_url moves authority to the hub for the task store; it should move authority for plugin policy too.

# Concrete deliverable

- Define and codify the rule in SPEC / SKILL.md: when master_url is set, .balls/plugins/ on the hub is authoritative; the project repo's .balls/plugins/ is ignored (or refused with a clear error if present, so it can't drift)
- Decide on the storage shape: most natural is to read .balls/plugins/ from the state-repo's HEAD of balls/tasks (or wherever plugin configs live on the hub), not from the project's committed config
- Implement the resolver: a single seam analogous to state_remote() / master_url() that returns the effective plugin config map
- Migration: a master_url repo that ALSO has project-side .balls/plugins/ at adoption time — warn? refuse? auto-migrate to the hub? Probably warn-and-ignore on first run, with a follow-on cleanup helper

# Out of scope

- Per-task plugin overrides (different question, different ball if it grows legs)
- Plugins on the hub talking to multiple master_url clients with different auth contexts — that's the bridge-pattern concern, already documented under bl-bfb7

# Origin

User directive on bl-ffb4 close-out: "on 5, the master wins. The idea is unified config. file a ball for that".
title: Plugin precedence on mastered repos: master wins (unified config)
